controls.control_title: Fairness
controls.control_text_full: AI systems should be designed and operated to avoid unfair bias, discrimination, or harm to individuals or groups, ensuring equitable outcomes across diverse populations, particularly with respect to protected attributes such as gender, race, ethnicity, religion, and socioeconomic status. [...]
controls.intent_summary: AI systems must be developed and deployed to prevent discriminatory outcomes and ensure fair treatment for all individuals, especially marginalized groups. Developers must actively identify and mitigate biases in data and algorithms.
evidence_signals: Bias audit reports for training datasets
Fairness metrics documented in model cards
Diverse testing cohorts used in validation
evidence_anti_signals: Outcomes show statistically significant disparities by protected group
controls.remediation_template: Conduct a comprehensive bias assessment across all training data and model outputs using recognized fairness metrics. Implement corrective measures such as re-sampling, re-weighting, or algorithmic adjustments, and document the process for auditability.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Privacy & Security
controls.control_text_full: AI systems must protect personal and sensitive data throughout their lifecycle, ensuring compliance with data protection laws, minimizing data collection, employing strong encryption, and implementing access controls to prevent unauthorized use or breaches. [...]
controls.intent_summary: AI systems must safeguard personal and sensitive data by design, limiting collection, enforcing strict access controls, and using encryption to prevent misuse or exposure. Compliance with data protection laws is mandatory.
evidence_signals: Encryption applied at rest and in transit
Access logs for authorized users maintained
evidence_anti_signals: No role-based access controls in place
controls.remediation_template: Implement end-to-end data protection measures including encryption, access controls, and data minimization. Conduct regular security audits and ensure compliance with KSA’s data protection regulations and international standards.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Humanity
controls.control_text_full: AI systems must be designed to respect human dignity, autonomy, and rights, ensuring that humans remain in control of critical decisions and are not replaced or manipulated by AI in ways that undermine human agency or fundamental freedoms. [...]
controls.intent_summary: AI must augment, not replace, human decision-making—especially in high-stakes contexts. Systems must preserve human oversight and prevent manipulation or erosion of autonomy.
evidence_signals: Clear escalation paths for AI decisions
User consent mechanisms for AI interactions
evidence_anti_signals: Fully automated decisions in sensitive domains
AI systems designed to manipulate behavior
controls.remediation_template: Ensure all AI systems involving critical decisions include meaningful human review options. Design interfaces that preserve user agency and prohibit deceptive or coercive interactions.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Social & Environmental Benefits
controls.control_text_full: AI systems should be developed to promote social well-being and environmental sustainability, avoiding harm to communities and ecosystems, and actively contributing to equitable development, public health, education, and climate resilience. [...]
controls.intent_summary: AI systems must deliver measurable societal and environmental benefits, avoiding harm to communities or ecosystems, and prioritizing use cases that advance public good and sustainability.
evidence_signals: Impact assessments linking AI to SDGs
Use cases targeting public health or education
evidence_anti_signals: AI deployed solely for profit without social benefit
Systems exacerbating inequality or resource depletion
controls.remediation_template: Conduct a social and environmental impact assessment for each AI system, prioritizing use cases that advance equity, health, or sustainability. Document outcomes and adjust deployment to maximize positive externalities.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

related_icaire_terms.term_en: Association for the Advancement of Artificial Intelligence (AAAI)

controls.control_title: Reliability & Safety
controls.control_text_full: AI systems must operate reliably under expected conditions, with robust testing, validation, and monitoring to prevent failures that could cause physical, psychological, or economic harm to users or third parties. [...]
controls.intent_summary: AI systems must be rigorously tested and continuously monitored to ensure consistent, safe performance under real-world conditions, minimizing risks of failure or unintended harm.
evidence_signals: Test reports for edge cases and failure modes
Real-time monitoring dashboards for model drift
evidence_anti_signals: System continues operating despite known failures
controls.remediation_template: Implement comprehensive testing for edge cases, model drift, and failure scenarios. Deploy real-time monitoring and automated fail-safes, and establish protocols for immediate system shutdown if safety thresholds are breached.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Transparency & Explainability
controls.control_text_full: AI systems must provide clear, accessible explanations of their logic, decisions, and limitations to users and affected parties, enabling understanding, trust, and accountability, particularly when decisions impact rights or opportunities. [...]
controls.intent_summary: Users and affected individuals must be able to understand how AI systems reach decisions, especially when those decisions affect their rights, opportunities, or well-being.
evidence_signals: Model documentation including assumptions and limitations
evidence_anti_signals: Technical jargon used to obscure decision logic
controls.remediation_template: Provide clear, non-technical explanations of AI decisions to end-users. Document model logic, data sources, and limitations. Use interpretable models or post-hoc explainability tools where black-box models are unavoidable.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Accountability & Responsibility
controls.control_text_full: Organizations and individuals involved in the AI lifecycle must be clearly accountable for the design, development, deployment, and outcomes of AI systems, with defined roles, governance structures, and mechanisms for redress when harm occurs. [...]
controls.intent_summary: Clear accountability must be assigned at every stage of the AI lifecycle, with defined roles, governance oversight, and accessible redress mechanisms for individuals harmed by AI systems.
evidence_anti_signals: No process for reporting AI-related harm
Blame shifted between teams without ownership
controls.remediation_template: Assign clear accountability roles (e.g., AI Ethics Officer), establish a governance body to oversee compliance, and implement a transparent, accessible process for individuals to report harm and seek remedy.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Little or no risk AI systems
controls.control_text_full: There are no restrictions on AI systems that pose little or no risk such as spam filters, but it is recommended that these systems be ethically compliant. [...]
controls.intent_summary: Even low-risk AI systems should follow ethical principles as a best practice, though they are not subject to formal compliance requirements.
evidence_signals: Ethical compliance checklist applied to low-risk systems
evidence_anti_signals: No ethical review for any system regardless of risk
Low-risk systems treated as exempt from all ethics
controls.remediation_template: Apply voluntary ethical guidelines to all AI systems, regardless of risk level, to foster a culture of responsibility. Document the rationale for risk classification and maintain records for audit.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Limited risk AI systems
controls.control_text_full: AI systems that pose limited risks, such as technical programs related to function, development, and performance, are subject to the application of the AI ethics principles mentioned in this document. [...]
controls.intent_summary: Systems with limited risk must fully apply all seven AI ethics principles, though they do not require formal pre-deployment conformity assessments.
evidence_signals: Internal review logs for ethics alignment
No formal certification but documented compliance
evidence_anti_signals: Ethics principles ignored for 'limited risk' systems
controls.remediation_template: Ensure all limited-risk systems undergo internal ethics review against all seven principles. Document the review process and retain evidence of compliance for audit purposes.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: High risk AI systems
controls.control_text_full: AI systems that pose 'high risks' to basic rights must undergo pre- and post-conformity assessments, and in addition to adhering to ethics, the relevant statutory requirements must be considered. [...]
controls.intent_summary: High-risk AI systems require formal conformity assessments before and after deployment, alongside compliance with legal and regulatory requirements beyond ethics principles.
evidence_signals: Cross-reference to statutory compliance (e.g., data law)
evidence_anti_signals: Ethics applied but legal requirements ignored
controls.remediation_template: Conduct mandatory pre- and post-deployment conformity assessments for high-risk systems. Align with all applicable KSA laws and regulations, and retain audit-ready documentation of compliance.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Unacceptable risk AI systems
controls.control_text_full: AI systems that pose an 'unacceptable risk' to people’s safety, livelihood, and rights such as those related to social profiling, exploitation of children, or distortion of behavior that are likely to occur are not allowed. [...]
controls.intent_summary: AI systems that threaten fundamental rights—such as social scoring, child exploitation, or behavioral manipulation—are strictly prohibited and must not be developed or deployed.
evidence_signals: Screening process to block unacceptable use cases
evidence_anti_signals: Prohibited systems in development or use
No screening mechanism for high-risk applications
controls.remediation_template: Immediately halt development or deployment of any system falling under unacceptable risk categories. Implement a mandatory screening protocol during project intake to block prohibited use cases.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Plan and Design Phase Requirements
controls.control_text_full: Plan and Design: Define the problem Support your problem with a data-driven approach Select a framing approach on technology and system which governs AI Conduct feasibility assessment for the selected approach Define KPIs
controls.intent_summary: Ensure AI projects begin with clearly defined problems, data-driven justification, feasible technical approaches, and measurable success metrics aligned with ethical goals.
evidence_signals: Problem statement documented with business and ethical rationale
Feasibility assessment report including ethical risks
KPIs linked to fairness, privacy, and performance
evidence_anti_signals: KPIs focused only on accuracy or efficiency
controls.remediation_template: Require all AI projects to document a problem statement, feasibility analysis, and ethical KPIs before initiation. Ensure planning includes stakeholder impact assessment and alignment with OECD principles.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Prepare Input Data Requirements
controls.control_text_full: Prepare Input Data: Gather data Discover and assess data Cleanse and validate data Transform data into AI model input features Following the best practice of responsible data acquisition, handling, classification, and management must be a priority to ensure that results and outcomes align with the AI system’s set goals and objectives.
controls.intent_summary: Ensure data used in AI systems is responsibly acquired, cleansed of bias, representative, and managed to avoid reinforcing discrimination or inaccuracies.
evidence_signals: Bias detection reports during data cleansing
Documentation of data representation across demographics
evidence_anti_signals: No data quality or bias assessment performed
Data sourced without consent or transparency
controls.remediation_template: Implement data governance protocols including provenance tracking, bias audits, and representation analysis before model training. Reject datasets with known biases unless mitigated and justified.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Build and Validate Phase Requirements
controls.control_text_full: Build and Validate: Train and test the model Tune the hyperparameters of the model Validate model performance Risk Evaluation At the build and validate stage of the AI System Lifecycle, it is essential to take into consideration implementation fairness as a common theme when building, testing, and implementing the AI system.
controls.intent_summary: Ensure model development includes fairness testing, performance validation, and risk evaluation—selecting models based on both accuracy and ethical metrics.
evidence_signals: Model selection log including fairness trade-offs
Validation reports showing performance across subgroups
controls.remediation_template: Require fairness and performance metrics to be jointly evaluated during model selection. Document trade-offs and justify model choices based on ethical impact, not just technical accuracy.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

related_icaire_terms.term_en: Validation Dataset (or Validation Data, Validation Set)

controls.control_title: Deploy and Monitor Phase Requirements
controls.control_text_full: Deploy and Monitor: Deploy the model to the AI system Create versioning structure Monitor the production model performance periodically Assess if there is a need to change the design according to results of periodic reviews Well-defined mechanisms and protocols should be set in place when deploying the AI system to measure the fairness and performance of the outcomes...
controls.intent_summary: Continuously monitor deployed AI systems for performance drift and fairness violations, with protocols to trigger retraining or retirement based on observed impacts.
evidence_signals: Production monitoring dashboard with fairness and accuracy metrics
Version control logs for model updates
Incident logs for fairness deviations and remediation actions
controls.remediation_template: Establish continuous monitoring of deployed AI systems with alerts for fairness or accuracy drift. Maintain version control, document review cycles, and define triggers for model retraining or decommissioning.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Respect for privacy and protection of personal data
controls.control_text_full: modelled in a manner such that there is respect for the protection of the privacy of individuals, personal data is not misused and exploited, and the decision criteria of the automated technology is not based on personally identifying characteristics or information. 2- The use of personal information should be limited only to that which is necessary for the proper functioning of the system. [...]
controls.intent_summary: AI systems must be designed to protect individual privacy, avoid misuse of personal data, and refrain from using personally identifiable traits in decision-making. Data use must be minimal and necessary.
evidence_signals: No use of SSN, name, or biometrics in model inputs
evidence_anti_signals: Use of full names or IDs in training data
controls.remediation_template: Implement data minimization protocols, remove personally identifiable information from training datasets, and document justification for all data fields used. Conduct regular privacy impact assessments.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Approval for profiling and ethical compliance
controls.control_text_full: The design of AI systems resulting in the profiling of individuals or communities may only occur if approved by Chief Compliance and Ethics Officer, Compliance Officer or in compliance with a code of ethics and conduct developed by a national regulatory authority for the specific sector or industry.
controls.intent_summary: Profiling via AI requires formal ethical approval from designated officers or adherence to a sector-specific code of ethics approved by a regulatory authority.
evidence_signals: Reference to approved sectoral ethics code
evidence_anti_signals: No ethics code referenced or adopted
controls.remediation_template: Establish a formal approval workflow for profiling systems, designate authorized officers, and align with recognized sectoral ethics codes. Maintain audit logs of all approvals.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Security blueprint aligned to best practices
controls.control_text_full: The security and protection blueprint of the AI system, including the data to be processed and the algorithm to be used, should be aligned to best practices to be able to withstand cyberattacks and data breach attempts.
controls.intent_summary: AI systems must follow established cybersecurity best practices to defend against attacks and data breaches, covering both data and algorithmic components.
evidence_signals: Use of NIST or ISO 27001 controls
evidence_anti_signals: Use of default or unpatched components
controls.remediation_template: Adopt recognized security frameworks (e.g., NIST), document system architecture, conduct regular penetration testing, and ensure all components are patched and hardened.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Data classification and protection levels
controls.control_text_full: All processed data should be classified to ensure that it receives the appropriate level of protection in accordance with its sensitivity or security classification... Data classification should be conducted in a contextual manner that does not result in the inference of personal information.
controls.intent_summary: Data must be classified by sensitivity and handled with corresponding protections; classification must avoid inferring personal details indirectly.
evidence_signals: Access controls tied to classification levels
No inferred PII from aggregated data
evidence_anti_signals: All data treated as public or unclassified
Inferred sensitive attributes from non-sensitive inputs
controls.remediation_template: Implement a formal data classification policy with defined levels (e.g., public, internal, confidential), enforce access controls, and audit for indirect inference of personal data.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: De-identification mechanisms for sensitive data
controls.control_text_full: Security mechanisms for de-identification should be planned for the sensitive or personal data in the system. Furthermore, read/write/update actions should be authorized for the relevant groups.
controls.intent_summary: Sensitive data must be de-identified using robust techniques, and access to data must be strictly controlled by role-based authorization.
evidence_signals: Use of k-anonymity or differential privacy
evidence_anti_signals: Raw PII stored in training datasets
All users have full read/write access
controls.remediation_template: Apply industry-standard de-identification techniques (e.g., masking, anonymization) and enforce least-privilege access controls with audit trails for all data actions.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Human-centric design and ethical alignment
controls.control_text_full: AI systems should be built using an ethical methodology to be just and ethically permissible, based on intrinsic and fundamental human rights and cultural values... Predictive models should not be designed to deceive, manipulate, or condition behavior...
controls.intent_summary: AI must respect human rights and cultural values, avoid manipulative design, and empower human autonomy rather than undermine it.
evidence_signals: Design documented to support human choice
No dark patterns or behavioral manipulation
evidence_anti_signals: System designed to maximize engagement at user expense
No reference to human rights in design docs
controls.remediation_template: Integrate human rights impact assessments into design, avoid manipulative UI/UX patterns, and ensure AI augments rather than replaces human judgment and autonomy.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Social and environmental benefit orientation
controls.control_text_full: AI systems should neither cause nor accelerate harm... but rather contribute to empowering and complementing social and environmental progress while addressing associated social and environmental ills.
controls.intent_summary: AI must actively promote social and environmental well-being and avoid contributing to harm, inequality, or ecological degradation.
evidence_signals: Impact assessment linking AI to SDGs
evidence_anti_signals: AI increases resource consumption without justification
No assessment of societal or environmental impact
controls.remediation_template: Align AI objectives with sustainable development goals, measure environmental footprint (e.g., energy use), and document societal benefits achieved or intended.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

related_icaire_terms.term_en: Association for the Advancement of Artificial Intelligence (AAAI)

controls.control_title: Continuous monitoring of societal and environmental impact
controls.control_text_full: After the deployment of the AI system, the AI System Owner should ensure that continuous assessment of the human, social, cultural, economic and environmental impact of AI technologies are carried out...
controls.intent_summary: AI systems require ongoing evaluation of their societal and environmental effects post-deployment to ensure alignment with intended benefits and mitigate unintended harms.
evidence_anti_signals: Impact assessments conducted only at launch
controls.remediation_template: Establish a formal, recurring process to assess societal, cultural, and environmental impacts, engage stakeholders, and adapt systems based on findings.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Data pertaining to social and environmental topics must be accessible to public infrastructure and articulate social benefit
controls.control_text_full: The data pertaining to the social and environmental topics should be accessible to the public data infrastructure and must clearly articulate the social benefit of the data presented. [...]
controls.intent_summary: AI developers must ensure social and environmental data used in systems is publicly accessible and explicitly demonstrates tangible societal benefit.
evidence_signals: Public data portal listing used datasets with benefit statements
Documentation linking data sources to SDG outcomes
Third-party audit confirming data accessibility and benefit claims
evidence_anti_signals: Data locked behind paywalls or NDAs
No documentation of social benefit from data use
controls.remediation_template: Establish a public data registry with clear metadata linking datasets to societal outcomes, and document how each contributes to recognized social or environmental goals. Ensure access is non-discriminatory and auditable.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: AI models must aim for socially recognized ends with demonstrable transformative impact
controls.control_text_full: The models and algorithms must have, as their ultimate goal, a result linked to a socially recognized end, with the ability to demonstrate how the expected results relate to that social or environmental purpose through transformative and impactful benefits where applicable. [...]
controls.intent_summary: AI systems must be designed to achieve clearly defined social or environmental goals, with evidence showing how they create meaningful, measurable impact.
evidence_signals: Project charter linking AI purpose to SDGs or public policy
Impact assessment reports showing pre/post outcomes
evidence_anti_signals: Vague mission statements without measurable outcomes
No linkage between model output and societal benefit
controls.remediation_template: Define and document specific, measurable social or environmental objectives for each AI system, and validate alignment through impact assessments and stakeholder review prior to deployment.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: AI systems must maintain sustainable resource usage and ecological responsibility
controls.control_text_full: It is best practice to measure and maintain acceptable levels of resource usage and energy consumption during this phase setting the tone that AI systems not only strive to foster AI solutions that address global concerns relating to social and environmental issues but also practice sustainable and ecological responsibilities. [...]
controls.intent_summary: Developers must monitor and limit the energy and resource consumption of AI systems to ensure ecological sustainability alongside functional goals.
evidence_signals: Energy consumption metrics per inference or training run
Carbon footprint reports for AI model lifecycle
Use of energy-efficient hardware or cloud providers
evidence_anti_signals: No tracking of computational resource use
Use of high-energy models without justification
controls.remediation_template: Implement monitoring tools to track energy use and carbon emissions across the AI lifecycle, and adopt efficiency best practices such as model pruning, efficient architectures, and green cloud hosting.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Continuous assessment of AI’s human, social, cultural, economic and environmental impact post-deployment
controls.control_text_full: After the deployment of the AI system, the AI System Owner should ensure that continuous assessment of the human, social, cultural, economic and environmental impact of AI technologies are carried out with full cognizance of the implications of the AI system for sustainability as a set of constantly evolving goals across a range of dimensions against the priority objectives that were set at the Plan and Design phase. [...]
controls.intent_summary: AI owners must continuously monitor real-world impacts across multiple dimensions and align them with original design goals to ensure ongoing societal alignment.
evidence_signals: Dashboard tracking KPIs across social/environmental metrics
evidence_anti_signals: Impact assessments conducted only once at launch
controls.remediation_template: Establish a formal, ongoing monitoring framework with defined metrics, stakeholder feedback channels, and periodic reporting to evaluate AI’s real-world impacts and adjust systems accordingly.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: AI systems must be designed to withstand uncertainty, instability, and volatility
controls.control_text_full: Designing and developing an AI system that can withstand the uncertainty, instability, and volatility that it might encounter is crucial. Planning to set out a robust and reliable AI system that works with different sets of inputs and situations is essential to prevent unintended harm and mitigate risks of system failures when positioned against unknown and unforeseen events. [...]
controls.intent_summary: AI systems must be engineered for resilience under unpredictable conditions to avoid harm from unexpected inputs or environmental changes.
evidence_signals: Stress test logs under edge-case scenarios
evidence_anti_signals: Testing only on ideal or clean data
No documentation of system behavior under stress
controls.remediation_template: Conduct comprehensive stress testing across diverse, adversarial, and edge-case inputs; document failure modes and implement fallback mechanisms to ensure safe operation under volatility.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Establish standards and protocols for assessing AI system reliability and safety
controls.control_text_full: Establishing a set of standards and protocols for assessing the reliability of an AI system is necessary to secure the safety of the system’s algorithm and data output. It is essential to keep a sustainable technical outlay and outcomes generated from the system to maintain the public’s trust and confidence in the AI system. [...]
controls.intent_summary: Organizations must define and enforce formal standards to evaluate and certify AI reliability and safety before and after deployment.
evidence_signals: Safety compliance checklist signed by engineers
evidence_anti_signals: Reliability claims based on anecdotal evidence
controls.remediation_template: Develop and publish a standardized reliability and safety assessment protocol, including test cases, pass/fail criteria, and independent verification procedures for all AI systems.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: AI systems must be technically sound, robust, and protected against malicious exploitation
controls.control_text_full: The AI system should be technically sound, robust, and developed to prevent malicious usage to exploit its data and outcomes to harm entities, individuals or communities. [...]
controls.intent_summary: AI systems must be hardened against adversarial attacks and misuse, ensuring data and outputs cannot be weaponized to cause harm.
evidence_signals: Access control logs and data usage policies
evidence_anti_signals: Open APIs exposing sensitive model outputs
controls.remediation_template: Implement security-by-design practices including input validation, model watermarking, access controls, and regular adversarial testing to prevent exploitation of AI systems.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Measure data quality, accuracy, suitability, and credibility before use
controls.control_text_full: Adequate steps and actions should be taken to measure the data sample’s quality, accuracy, suitability, and credibility when dealing with the data sets of an AI model. This is essential to ensure the accuracy of data interpretation by the AI system, the consistency of avoiding misleading measurements, as well as ensuring the relevance of the AI system’s outcomes to the purpose of the model. [...]
controls.intent_summary: Data used in AI systems must be rigorously evaluated for quality and relevance to prevent biased, inaccurate, or misleading outcomes.
evidence_anti_signals: Use of unvetted public datasets without analysis
No documentation of data selection criteria
controls.remediation_template: Implement a data intake protocol requiring quality scoring, bias detection, and relevance validation before any dataset is used in training or inference.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Stress test AI systems under outlier and extreme scenarios
controls.control_text_full: It is crucial for the build and validate step to test how the system behaves under outlier events, extreme parameters, etc. In this step, stress test data should be prepared for extreme scenarios. [...]
controls.intent_summary: AI systems must be tested under extreme, rare, or adversarial conditions to ensure they do not fail dangerously or produce harmful outputs.
evidence_signals: Stress test datasets with extreme values
Failure rate logs under edge conditions
evidence_anti_signals: No record of outlier testing performed
controls.remediation_template: Design and execute stress tests using adversarial, rare, and boundary-condition data; document system behavior and implement safeguards for unsafe outputs.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

related_icaire_terms.term_en: Test Dataset (or Test Data, Test Set)

controls.control_title: Require human oversight for irreversible or life-and-death AI decisions
controls.control_text_full: Automated AI systems involving scenarios where decisions are understood to have an impact that is irreversible or difficult to reverse or may involve life-and-death decisions should trigger human oversight and final determination. [...]
controls.intent_summary: AI systems making high-stakes, irreversible decisions must include mandatory human review and final approval to prevent autonomous harm.
evidence_signals: Policy requiring override for critical decisions
evidence_anti_signals: Fully automated decisions in healthcare, justice, or safety contexts
controls.remediation_template: Implement mandatory human review checkpoints for any AI system making decisions with irreversible consequences, and log all overrides and justifications.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Prohibit AI use for social scoring or mass surveillance
controls.control_text_full: Furthermore, AI systems should not be used for social scoring or mass surveillance purposes. [...]
controls.intent_summary: AI systems must never be deployed for social scoring or mass surveillance, as these violate fundamental rights and ethical boundaries.
evidence_signals: Procurement contracts excluding surveillance use cases
Ethics review board rejection of such proposals
evidence_anti_signals: Use of AI to rate citizens’ behavior or trustworthiness
Deployment of facial recognition for public monitoring without consent
controls.remediation_template: Enact and enforce a strict organizational policy prohibiting any use of AI for social scoring or mass surveillance, and train teams to recognize and reject such proposals.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

related_icaire_terms.term_en: Association for the Advancement of Artificial Intelligence (AAAI)

controls.control_title: Ensure documentation standards to track system evolution and fix vulnerabilities
controls.control_text_full: The documentation standards are essential to track the evolution of the system, foresee possible risks and fix vulnerabilities. [...]
controls.intent_summary: All AI system changes, decisions, and tests must be thoroughly documented to enable traceability, risk forecasting, and vulnerability remediation.
evidence_signals: Version-controlled model and data lineage logs
evidence_anti_signals: No versioning of models or datasets
Oral decisions undocumented in system records
controls.remediation_template: Implement a centralized documentation system tracking model versions, data sources, test results, and decisions, ensuring full auditability and rapid vulnerability response.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Critical design decisions require stakeholder sign-off
controls.control_text_full: All critical decision points in the system design should be subject to sign-off by relevant stakeholders to minimize risks and make stakeholders accountable for the decisions. [...]
controls.intent_summary: Key design choices in AI systems must be formally approved by cross-functional stakeholders to ensure accountability and risk awareness.
evidence_signals: RACI matrix for AI development phases
evidence_anti_signals: Design decisions made by single engineers without review
controls.remediation_template: Establish a formal gatekeeping process requiring documented sign-off from legal, ethical, technical, and user-representative stakeholders before advancing AI system phases.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Continuously monitor AI system robustness and safety post-deployment
controls.control_text_full: Monitoring the robustness of the AI system should be adopted and undertaken in a periodic and continuous manner to measure and assess any risks related to the technicalities of the AI system (an inward perspective) as well as the magnitude of the risk posed by the system and its capabilities (an outward perspective). [...]
controls.intent_summary: AI systems must be continuously monitored for both internal technical stability and external societal risks after deployment.
controls.remediation_template: Deploy automated monitoring tools tracking model drift, performance decay, and societal impact indicators, with escalation protocols for anomalies.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: AI systems must be transparent and explainable to affected parties
controls.control_text_full: AI systems must be built with a high level of clarity and explainability as well as features to track the stages of automated decision-making, particularly those that may lead to detrimental effects on data subjects. [...]
controls.intent_summary: AI systems must provide clear, accessible explanations of their decisions to individuals affected by them, enabling understanding and redress.
evidence_signals: Explanations provided to users at point of decision
User-facing documentation on how decisions are made
evidence_anti_signals: Black-box models with no explanation capability
Users denied access to decision rationale
controls.remediation_template: Integrate explainability features (e.g., SHAP, LIME, or plain-language summaries) into AI interfaces and ensure users can request and receive understandable justifications for decisions.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Ensure transparency of data, algorithms, processes, and purpose to affected stakeholders
controls.control_text_full: It follows that data, algorithms, capabilities, processes, and purpose of the AI system need to be transparent and communicated as well as explainable to those who are directly and indirectly affected. [...]
controls.intent_summary: All components of an AI system — data, logic, purpose — must be disclosed in accessible terms to anyone impacted by its use.
evidence_signals: Open documentation of training data sources
Stakeholder Q&A portals on AI functionality
evidence_anti_signals: Proprietary claims blocking disclosure of model inputs
No public information on system purpose or limitations
controls.remediation_template: Create and maintain public-facing documentation detailing data sources, algorithmic purpose, limitations, and decision logic, tailored to audience technical levels.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Ensure traceability of data and decision processes for transparency
controls.control_text_full: The data sets and the processes that yield the AI system’s decision should be documented to the best possible standard to allow for traceability and an increase in transparency. [...]
controls.intent_summary: All data inputs and decision pathways in AI systems must be fully documented to enable auditing and accountability.
evidence_signals: Decision log with timestamps and inputs
evidence_anti_signals: No record of how inputs led to outputs
controls.remediation_template: Implement end-to-end data and decision logging, ensuring every input, transformation, and output is traceable to support audits and user inquiries.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Conduct AI ethics due diligence on third-party systems before procurement
controls.control_text_full: If the AI system is built by a third party, AI system owners should make sure that an AI Ethics due diligence is carried out and all the documentation are accessible and traceable before procurement or sign-off. [...]
controls.intent_summary: Organizations must verify third-party AI systems meet ethical standards through due diligence before acquisition or deployment.
evidence_anti_signals: Vendor refuses to share model cards or test results
controls.remediation_template: Require all third-party AI procurements to include ethics compliance documentation, including bias tests, transparency reports, and data governance policies, before contract signing.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Provide accessible information on AI model decisions to users and public
controls.control_text_full: The AI system should be designed to include an information section in the platform to give an overview of the AI model decisions as part of the overall transparency application of the technology. [...]
controls.intent_summary: AI interfaces must include clear, accessible information explaining how decisions are made, available to users upon request or publicly.
evidence_signals: In-app help section on AI decision logic
Public FAQ or explainer page for AI system
controls.remediation_template: Embed a user-friendly explanation module within AI interfaces, allowing users to easily access how decisions were reached, in plain language and context.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Establish mechanism to log and address complaints transparently
controls.control_text_full: The model should establish a process mechanism to log and address issues and complaints that arise to be able to resolve them in a transparent and explainable manner. [...]
controls.intent_summary: AI systems must have a public, traceable process for receiving, logging, and resolving user complaints in an open and explainable way.
evidence_signals: Public complaint portal with response timelines
evidence_anti_signals: Complaints ignored or deleted without record
controls.remediation_template: Create a public-facing complaint and feedback system with clear submission, tracking, and resolution procedures, and publish anonymized summaries of actions taken.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Transparency in AI design and output
controls.control_text_full: Transparency in AI is thought about from two perspectives, the first is the process behind it (the design and implementation practices that lead to an algorithmically supported outcome) and the second is in terms of its product (the content and justification of that outcome). Algorithms should be developed in a transparent way to ensure that input transparency is evident and explainable to the end-users of the AI system to be able to provide evidence and information on the data used to process the decisions that have been processed. [...]
controls.intent_summary: AI systems must be transparent in both their design process and decision outputs, enabling users to understand how data and algorithms lead to outcomes. This ensures accountability and informed interaction.
evidence_signals: Documentation of data sources and preprocessing steps
evidence_anti_signals: No documentation of training data provenance
Black-box models with no interpretability features
controls.remediation_template: Implement explainable AI (XAI) techniques and document all design and data decisions. Provide clear, accessible explanations to users about how decisions are made, and maintain audit trails of model inputs and outputs for review.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Data quality and bias mitigation
controls.control_text_full: Data sets should be assessed in the context of their accuracy, suitability, validity, and source. [...] Data sets should be approved and signed-off before commencing with developing the AI model. Furthermore, the data should be cleansed from societal biases. In parallel with the fairness principle, the sensitive features should not be included in the model data. [...]
controls.intent_summary: Data used to train AI systems must be validated for quality, legality, and fairness. Biases must be identified and removed, and any use of sensitive attributes must be justified and documented.
evidence_signals: Bias audit reports with mitigation actions
Documentation of sensitive feature exclusion rationale
Data lineage records from collection to training
Cleaned dataset versions with version control
evidence_anti_signals: No record of bias testing or mitigation
Use of protected attributes without justification
controls.remediation_template: Establish formal data validation and bias assessment protocols before model development. Document all decisions regarding sensitive attributes, cleanse datasets for discriminatory patterns, and retain signed-off data approval records for audit.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

related_icaire_terms.term_en: Validation Dataset (or Validation Data, Validation Set)
Test Dataset (or Test Data, Test Set)

controls.control_title: Accountability across AI lifecycle
controls.control_text_full: The accountability and responsibility principle holds designers, vendors, procurers, developers, owners and assessors of AI systems and the technology itself ethically responsible and liable for the decisions and actions that may result in potential risk and negative effects on individuals and communities. [...]
controls.intent_summary: All parties involved in the AI lifecycle must be clearly accountable for harms caused by AI systems, whether intended or not. Responsibility must be assigned and maintained from design to deployment.
evidence_signals: Signed responsibility matrices per lifecycle phase
Named individuals accountable for model outcomes
Incident response plans with assigned owners
Audit trails linking decisions to responsible actors
evidence_anti_signals: Vague or undefined roles in AI governance
No named accountable parties for system failures
Contractual terms that disclaim all liability
controls.remediation_template: Define and document clear accountability assignments for every stage of the AI lifecycle. Ensure legal and ethical responsibility is assigned to specific roles, and embed liability obligations in procurement and development contracts.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Human oversight and intervention
controls.control_text_full: It is essential to build and design a human-controlled AI system where decisions on the processes and functionality of the technology are monitored and executed, and are susceptible to intervention from authorized users. Human governance and oversight establish the necessary control and levels of autonomy through set mechanisms.
controls.intent_summary: AI systems must be designed to allow meaningful human oversight and intervention at critical decision points, ensuring humans retain ultimate control and can override automated outcomes.
evidence_signals: Manual override buttons or escalation paths
Training for operators on intervention protocols
Logs of human overrides and decisions
System design specs requiring human review thresholds
evidence_anti_signals: Fully autonomous systems with no human review option
No training provided for human supervisors
controls.remediation_template: Design AI systems with mandatory human review checkpoints for high-risk decisions. Train operators to intervene, log all overrides, and ensure system architecture supports real-time human control without technical barriers.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Performance monitoring and alerting
controls.control_text_full: Upon deployment of the AI system, performance metrics relating the AI system’s output, accuracy and alignment to priorities and objectives, as well as its measured impact on individuals and communities should be documented, available and accessible to stakeholders. [...] Predefined triggers/alerts should be defined for this step on the data and performance metrics. [...] each trigger should be assigned to the appropriate stakeholder.
controls.intent_summary: Deployed AI systems must be continuously monitored using defined metrics, with automated alerts triggered by performance degradation or ethical risks, and assigned to responsible parties for response.
evidence_signals: Documented alert thresholds and escalation paths
Weekly/monthly performance reports shared with stakeholders
Incident logs tied to specific triggers and owners
Audit records of alert responses and resolutions
evidence_anti_signals: Alerts not assigned to any role
controls.remediation_template: Define and implement continuous monitoring with quantifiable KPIs and automated alerts tied to specific stakeholders. Document alert triggers, response protocols, and ensure regular review of system performance and incident logs.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Compliance oversight by SDAIA
controls.control_text_full: SDAIA works to review and update the principles of artificial intelligence ethics and monitor compliance with them. [...] Measure compliance of Adopting Entities on a regular basis based on the defined compliance mechanism directly or through sector regulators [...] and audit AI Ethics activities when required.
controls.intent_summary: A national authority (SDAIA) must regularly assess and audit organizational compliance with AI ethics principles, ensuring consistent application and enforcement across entities.
evidence_signals: Audit schedules and findings publicly accessible
evidence_anti_signals: Compliance reports not published or shared
controls.remediation_template: Establish a formal compliance monitoring program led by a national authority, requiring regular audits, public reporting, and enforcement mechanisms to verify adherence to AI ethics principles across all entities.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

related_icaire_terms.term_en: Association for the Advancement of Artificial Intelligence (AAAI)

controls.control_title: Designation of AI Ethics roles
controls.control_text_full: All Adopting Entities shall have the primary responsibility for ensuring that their AI Ethics documents are published in compliance with these AI Ethics Principles. [...] Head of the Entity / Chief Data Officer (CDO): Responsible for the AI Ethics practice within an Entity. [...] Chief Compliance Officer (CCO) / Compliance Officer (CO): The strategic lead of the AI Ethics practice [...]
controls.intent_summary: Each organization must designate specific roles (CDO, CCO) with clear responsibilities for implementing, monitoring, and reporting on AI ethics compliance across all AI activities.
evidence_signals: Organizational chart showing AI Ethics roles
Job descriptions for CDO/CCO with ethics duties
Signed AI Ethics Plan with assigned owners
Annual ethics reports submitted by designated leads
evidence_anti_signals: No named AI ethics lead in org structure
Ethics duties assigned to undefined or overloaded roles
controls.remediation_template: Formally designate and empower a Chief Data Officer and Chief Compliance Officer with documented authority and resources to lead AI ethics implementation, monitoring, and reporting within the organization.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: AI Fairness Position Statement
controls.control_text_full: A fairness position statement allows the owner of the AI technology to clearly state the fairness criteria that have been employed by the AI system and explain the rationale and logic behind it in a direct and non-technical language. [...] This document would be made accessible and available to the public and affected individuals and communities.
controls.intent_summary: AI developers must publish a clear, non-technical fairness statement explaining the ethical criteria used in the model and make it publicly accessible to affected stakeholders.
controls.remediation_template: Create and publish a clear, non-technical fairness position statement that explains the model’s ethical criteria, rationale, and decision logic, ensuring it is publicly accessible to affected individuals and communities.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Ethical Impact Assessment
controls.control_text_full: AI has accelerated innovation [...] it is imperative to evolve AI system ethical impact assessments to identify areas that need adjustment and recalibrating to design the AI model into an ethically accepted technology [...] evaluate and analyze the level of ethical impact [...] discriminatory harm impact and accurate representation [...] assess the ethical risks [...] act as a facilitator to address whether a model should move to production or deployment.
controls.intent_summary: Organizations must conduct ethical impact assessments to identify discriminatory harm, assess risks to communities, and determine if an AI system should proceed to deployment based on multi-stakeholder analysis.
controls.remediation_template: Conduct formal ethical impact assessments using multi-stakeholder input to evaluate discriminatory risks, document decision rationale for deployment, and ensure public confidence through transparent analysis.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Privacy and Security Standards
controls.control_text_full: Privacy and security standards are in place to help companies improve their information security strategy by providing guidelines and best practices based on the company’s industry and the type of data they maintain. [...] Examples: ISO 23894, NIST AI RMF, CIS Controls, PCI-DSS, COBIT.
controls.intent_summary: AI systems must comply with recognized privacy and security standards such as ISO 23894, NIST AI RMF, CIS Controls, PCI-DSS, or COBIT based on data type and industry context.
evidence_signals: Adoption of NIST AI RMF or ISO 23894
Security audit reports aligned to standards
evidence_anti_signals: Security controls not mapped to recognized frameworks
controls.remediation_template: Align AI system design and data handling with recognized standards (e.g., NIST AI RMF, ISO 23894, CIS Controls), document compliance mapping, and conduct audits against these frameworks.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Architecture for Trustworthy AI
controls.control_text_full: The requirements that are stated in the AI Ethics Principles and Controls section should be reflected in the design of the AI system architecture. [...] Sense: The system should be developed such that it recognizes all environmental elements [...] Algorithm Assessment: [...] ensure individuals or communities are informed about the use of algorithms [...] Plan: The system should only consider plans that adhere to the requirements. Act: The system’s actions should be restricted to behaviors that realize the requirements.
controls.intent_summary: AI system architecture must embed ethical requirements through a sense-plan-act framework, ensuring environmental awareness, algorithmic transparency, and action restrictions aligned with ethical rules.
evidence_signals: System design aligned with ethics controls
evidence_anti_signals: Actions not restricted by ethical rules
controls.remediation_template: Design AI systems using the sense-plan-act framework, ensure algorithmic transparency, restrict system actions to ethical boundaries, and embed human oversight at decision-critical points.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Fairness Assessment Tools
controls.control_text_full: It is a set of diagnostic methods that helps you compare how fair models and label markers perform for specific groups. It checks whether the model’s result is regularly overestimated or underestimated for one or more groups compared to others. Additionally, it evaluates how well the diversity of data is represented for each group. [...] Tool examples: Google Model Card Toolkit, AI Fairness 360, Microsoft Fairlearn, What-if Tool, Aequitas, Veritas, TensorFlow Fairness Indicators, AI Explainability 360.
controls.intent_summary: Organizations must use validated fairness assessment tools to detect performance disparities across demographic groups and ensure data diversity is adequately represented in AI models.
evidence_signals: Use of Fairlearn, Aequitas, or similar tools
Tool outputs integrated into model validation
controls.remediation_template: Integrate validated fairness assessment tools (e.g., Fairlearn, Aequitas) into model development to detect group-based disparities, document data diversity analysis, and implement corrective actions for biased outcomes.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: AI Methods Explanation Report: It should be explained why the system behaves the way it does and how it takes decisions.
controls.control_text_full: AI Methods Explanation Report: As explained under Transparency and Explainability section, it should be explained why the system behaves the way it does and how it takes decisions. Although some training methods have superior performance, they work as black-box, and it is a challenge to interpret the results. [...]
controls.intent_summary: AI systems must provide understandable explanations for their decisions, balancing performance with interpretability, and justifying the chosen level of explainability via cost-benefit analysis.
evidence_signals: Documentation of trade-offs between accuracy and interpretability
Use of XAI tools like AIX360 or SHAP
evidence_anti_signals: No explanation provided for high-stakes decisions
controls.remediation_template: Implement explainability techniques appropriate to risk level, document trade-offs between performance and interpretability, and ensure stakeholders can understand decision logic through accessible reports.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Algorithm Auditing: Unexpected algorithmic behaviors can be detected with algorithm audits.
controls.control_text_full: Algorithm Auditing: Unexpected algorithmic behaviors can be detected with algorithm audits. In general, algorithm audits are done on an ad-hoc basis, and it is important to standardize algorithm auditing process with supporting AI algorithms. The process should be systematic and continuous. [...]
controls.intent_summary: AI systems must undergo systematic, continuous audits to detect unethical or unexpected behaviors, with standardized processes aligned to ethical principles.
evidence_signals: Audit reports with findings and remediation plans
Scheduled audit cycles documented in governance plan
Use of third-party auditors for high-risk systems
evidence_anti_signals: No audit records or ad-hoc audits only
Audits limited to technical performance, not ethics
controls.remediation_template: Establish a formal, ongoing algorithmic audit program with defined scope, frequency, and criteria. Align audits with ethical principles and document findings, actions, and accountability.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Safety Self-Assessment: Safety considerations of accuracy, reliability, security, and robustness should be considered at every step of the AI System Lifecycle.
controls.control_text_full: Safety Self-Assessment: Safety considerations of accuracy, reliability, security, and robustness should be considered at every step of the AI System Lifecycle. AI system safety self-assessments should be continuously logged and documented in a way that allows review and re-assessment. [...]
controls.intent_summary: Organizations must conduct continuous, documented safety self-assessments across the AI lifecycle, evaluating design and implementation against safety objectives.
evidence_signals: Signed safety self-assessment checklists per lifecycle phase
Logs of safety reviews with dates and reviewers
Integration of safety metrics into CI/CD pipelines
controls.remediation_template: Implement mandatory, phase-specific safety self-assessments with standardized templates, assign ownership, and maintain an auditable log of assessments and corrective actions.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Data De-Identification is the process of eliminating Personally Identifiable Data (PII) from any document or other media...
controls.control_text_full: Data De-Identification is the process of eliminating Personally Identifiable Data (PII) from any document or other media, including an individual’s Protected Health Information (PHI). Data Anonymization is a kind of data sanitization process that intends to protect the privacy of individuals. [...]
controls.intent_summary: Personal data must be protected through technical methods like de-identification, anonymization, pseudonymization, or encryption, tailored to data sensitivity and regulatory requirements.
evidence_signals: Data classification schema with PII/PHI labels
evidence_anti_signals: Raw PII used in training datasets
No documentation of data protection methods applied
controls.remediation_template: Apply appropriate data protection techniques (e.g., pseudonymization, encryption) based on data sensitivity. Document methods used, validate effectiveness, and align with GDPR or equivalent standards.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Data Governance: Data Governance encompasses all aspects of data management throughout its entire life cycle...
controls.control_text_full: Data Governance: Data Governance encompasses all aspects of data management throughout its entire life cycle, including security, usability, availability, and privacy. This involves defining data handling policies and processes and assigning data management authority and responsibilities. [...]
controls.intent_summary: Organizations must establish clear governance structures for data management across its lifecycle, defining roles, policies, and accountability for security, privacy, and usability.
evidence_signals: Data governance charter with roles and responsibilities
Data handling policies aligned with regulations
Audit trails for data access and modifications
evidence_anti_signals: No defined data owner or steward
Policies exist but are not enforced or reviewed
controls.remediation_template: Define and document a data governance framework with assigned roles, lifecycle policies, and compliance checks. Ensure policies are communicated, trained, and audited regularly.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Data Minimization: Data minimization refers to the practice of collecting just the personal information required to satisfy a specified objective.
controls.control_text_full: Data Minimization: Data minimization refers to the practice of collecting just the personal information required to satisfy a specified objective. This helps to mitigate the risks connected with data breaches and the abuse of personal information.
controls.intent_summary: Only collect personal data strictly necessary for the stated purpose, reducing exposure and risk of misuse or breach.
evidence_signals: Data collection forms with justifications for each field
Audit logs showing only required data is stored
Retention policies aligned with purpose limitation
evidence_anti_signals: Collection of excessive or irrelevant personal data
No documented justification for data fields
controls.remediation_template: Review all data collection points to ensure only essential personal data is gathered. Document justification for each data element and enforce retention limits based on purpose.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: PD.1: Did you design the appropriate level of human oversight for the AI system and use case?
controls.control_text_full: PD.1: Did you design the appropriate level of human oversight for the AI system and use case? Does your AI system design prevent overconfidence in or overreliance on the AI system with necessary human intervention mechanisms? [...]
controls.intent_summary: AI systems must include designed human oversight mechanisms to prevent overreliance, ensure accountability, and enable intervention when needed.
evidence_anti_signals: Fully automated high-risk decisions without human review
No defined thresholds for human override
controls.remediation_template: Design and document human oversight protocols with clear triggers, roles, and KPIs. Train operators and integrate override mechanisms into system workflows for high-risk decisions.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: PD.10: Did you establish a strategy or a set of procedures to avoid creating or reinforcing unfair bias in the AI system...
controls.control_text_full: PD.10: Did you establish a strategy or a set of procedures to avoid creating or reinforcing unfair bias in the AI system, covering both input data as well as for the algorithm design?
controls.intent_summary: Organizations must proactively implement strategies to prevent bias in both training data and algorithm design, ensuring equitable outcomes across groups.
evidence_signals: Bias mitigation strategy documented in design phase
Data sourcing criteria to avoid skewed representation
Algorithmic fairness constraints applied during training
evidence_anti_signals: Bias prevention not addressed in design documents
Training data sourced from biased historical sources without correction
controls.remediation_template: Develop and implement a bias prevention strategy covering data selection, preprocessing, model training, and validation. Document controls and validate effectiveness across diverse groups.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: PD.14: Did you conduct an impact analysis on how the AI system affects fundamental human rights and cultural values?
controls.control_text_full: PD.14: Did you conduct an impact analysis on how the AI system affects fundamental human rights and cultural values? Did you list any potential negative effects on fundamental human rights and cultural values and the solutions or recovery mechanisms?
controls.intent_summary: AI systems must undergo human rights and cultural impact assessments to identify and mitigate potential harms to rights, dignity, and societal values.
evidence_signals: List of identified rights risks and mitigation plans
Stakeholder consultation records on cultural impact
evidence_anti_signals: Only technical risks assessed, ignoring human rights
controls.remediation_template: Conduct a structured human rights and cultural impact assessment prior to deployment. Document risks, affected groups, and mitigation or remediation plans with accountability.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: PD.16: Did you align your AI system with relevant standards or policies (for example, ISO, IEEE, Data Privacy Law)...
controls.control_text_full: PD.16: Did you align your AI system with relevant standards or policies (for example, ISO, IEEE, Data Privacy Law) or widely adopted protocols for daily data management and governance?
controls.intent_summary: AI systems must comply with recognized technical, ethical, and legal standards such as ISO, IEEE, GDPR, or national data protection laws.
evidence_signals: Mapping of system controls to ISO/IEC 24028 or IEEE 7000
Reference to national AI ethics policy in governance docs
evidence_anti_signals: No reference to any standards in documentation
controls.remediation_template: Map AI system design and operations to applicable international and national standards (e.g., ISO 24028, GDPR). Document alignment and update controls as standards evolve.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: PD.26: Did you design User Experience with human psychology in mind to avoid the risk of confusion, confirmation bias, or cognitive fatigue?
controls.control_text_full: PD.26: Did you design User Experience with human psychology in mind to avoid the risk of confusion, confirmation bias, or cognitive fatigue?
controls.intent_summary: AI interfaces must be designed to avoid exploiting cognitive biases or overwhelming users, ensuring ethical interaction and informed decision-making.
evidence_signals: UX usability testing reports with cognitive load metrics
Design guidelines addressing bias and fatigue
User feedback on clarity and trustworthiness of AI outputs
evidence_anti_signals: UI designed to maximize engagement over clarity
No user testing for psychological impact
controls.remediation_template: Apply human-centered design principles to AI interfaces, test for cognitive load and bias risks, and document UX decisions that prioritize clarity, trust, and user autonomy.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: PD.28: Has the data management approach been reviewed based on human-centric values and according to data regulations within the KSA?
controls.control_text_full: PD.28: Has the data management approach been reviewed based on human-centric values and according to data regulations within the KSA?
controls.intent_summary: Data management practices must be reviewed against human-centric ethical values and local data protection regulations, such as those in Saudi Arabia (KSA).
evidence_signals: Local regulatory compliance review report (e.g., KSA NCA)
Ethical review board approval for data use
Mapping of data practices to national AI policy
evidence_anti_signals: Only international standards referenced, ignoring local laws
No evidence of local regulatory review
controls.remediation_template: Review data management practices against local legal and ethical frameworks (e.g., KSA regulations). Document alignment and update policies to reflect jurisdictional requirements.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Assess likelihood of AI system causing harm or damage to users or third parties
controls.control_text_full: Did you assess whether there is a likelihood that the AI system may cause damage or harm to users or third parties? Did you assess the potential damage, impacted audience, and severity? Did you assess whether there is a likelihood that the AI system may unintentionally give wrong results or inaccurate predictions, fail or feed societal biases? Did you consider the potential impact or safety risk to the environment, living creatures, or society in addition to the Data Subjects? [...]
controls.intent_summary: Organizations must proactively evaluate potential harms, biases, and safety risks posed by AI systems to users, third parties, and society, including environmental and non-data-subject impacts.
evidence_signals: Risk assessment documentation identifying harm scenarios
Impact analysis reports on biased outcomes
Bias detection logs from testing phases
evidence_anti_signals: No evaluation of societal or environmental effects
controls.remediation_template: Develop and document a structured harm and bias assessment framework covering users, third parties, environment, and societal impacts. Integrate it into the AI lifecycle with defined thresholds and escalation paths.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Ensure AI system is interpretable and explainable
controls.control_text_full: Did you design an interpretable AI system where the data, algorithms, outcomes, and decisions are transparent and explainable to the related parties?
controls.intent_summary: AI systems must be designed so that their inputs, logic, and outputs can be understood and explained to stakeholders, enabling accountability and trust.
controls.remediation_template: Implement model-agnostic or intrinsic explainability techniques, document decision logic for stakeholders, and ensure explanations are accessible to non-technical users in context of use.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Design UX to avoid cognitive risks
controls.control_text_full: Did you design User Experience with human psychology in mind to avoid the risk of confusion, confirmation bias, or cognitive fatigue?
controls.intent_summary: AI interfaces must be designed to minimize psychological harm, such as manipulation through bias or overload, ensuring human autonomy and informed interaction.
evidence_signals: User feedback on clarity and fatigue
Design guidelines aligned with human factors
controls.remediation_template: Conduct human-centered design reviews focusing on cognitive load, decision fatigue, and bias risks. Integrate findings into UI/UX standards and validate with diverse user groups.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Establish privacy impact measurement mechanism
controls.control_text_full: Have you established a measurement or assessment mechanism for privacy impact?
controls.intent_summary: Organizations must implement measurable methods to evaluate how AI systems affect privacy, including data minimization, consent, and surveillance risks.
evidence_signals: Metrics tracking data access and retention
controls.remediation_template: Implement a standardized Privacy Impact Assessment (PIA) framework with quantifiable indicators, integrate it into the AI lifecycle, and conduct periodic reviews.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Review data management against human-centric values and KSA regulations
controls.control_text_full: Has the data management approach been reviewed based on human-centric values and according to data regulations within the KSA?
controls.intent_summary: Data handling practices must align with human rights principles and Saudi Arabia’s local data protection laws, ensuring cultural and legal compliance.
evidence_signals: Cross-reference of data practices with KSA regulations
Human rights impact notes in data governance
Data stewardship policies referencing KSA law
evidence_anti_signals: No cultural or ethical review records
controls.remediation_template: Align data management policies with Saudi Arabia’s data protection laws and human rights norms. Document legal and cultural alignment assessments for auditability.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Flag data privacy/protection issues in collection and processing
controls.control_text_full: Is there an established mechanism that flags issues related to data privacy or protection in the process of data collection and processing?
controls.intent_summary: Automated or procedural mechanisms must detect and alert on privacy violations during data ingestion and processing stages.
evidence_signals: Data classification tags with compliance checks
controls.remediation_template: Deploy automated privacy monitoring tools that trigger alerts for unauthorized data use, missing consent, or sensitive data exposure during ingestion and processing.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Ensure AI system security and data integrity under adversarial conditions
controls.control_text_full: Are there processes to ensure that AI systems are secure and keep information safe, confidential, and private, as well as the integrity of the processed information even under hostile or adversarial conditions?
controls.intent_summary: AI systems must be hardened against attacks, ensuring confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data and decisions even when targeted maliciously.
controls.remediation_template: Implement end-to-end security controls including encryption, access governance, adversarial robustness testing, and continuous monitoring for data tampering or model manipulation.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Assess data quality and source reliability
controls.control_text_full: Has the quality and source of the acquired data been assessed through set processes?
controls.intent_summary: Data used in AI systems must be evaluated for accuracy, provenance, and reliability using documented, repeatable processes.
controls.remediation_template: Establish a data quality framework with defined metrics (accuracy, completeness, freshness) and source vetting procedures, documented and reviewed at each lifecycle stage.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

related_icaire_terms.term_en: Validation Dataset (or Validation Data, Validation Set)
Test Dataset (or Test Data, Test Set)

controls.control_title: Assess post-training data analysis capability
controls.control_text_full: Has there been an assessment on whether an analysis can be performed post training and testing the data?
controls.intent_summary: Organizations must ensure that data remains analyzable after model training to support auditing, debugging, and compliance monitoring.
evidence_signals: Data retention policies aligned with audit needs
evidence_anti_signals: No retention policy for training data
controls.remediation_template: Preserve versioned training datasets and metadata for auditability. Ensure systems allow retrospective analysis without compromising privacy or security.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

related_icaire_terms.term_en: Test Dataset (or Test Data, Test Set)
Validation Dataset (or Validation Data, Validation Set)

controls.control_title: Consider diversity and inclusion in datasets
controls.control_text_full: Has diversity and inclusion of the dataset at hand been considered or reviewed?
controls.intent_summary: Training datasets must be evaluated for representation across demographic, geographic, and cultural dimensions to prevent bias and exclusion.
controls.remediation_template: Conduct regular diversity audits of training data using demographic and contextual metrics. Actively source underrepresented data and document inclusion efforts.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

related_icaire_terms.term_en: Training Dataset (or Training Data, Training Set)
Test Dataset (or Test Data, Test Set)
Validation Dataset (or Validation Data, Validation Set)

controls.control_title: Analyze proxies for sensitive features
controls.control_text_full: Has an analysis process been developed for the proxies of the sensitive features?
controls.intent_summary: Indirect indicators of sensitive attributes (e.g., zip code as proxy for race) must be identified and mitigated to prevent discriminatory outcomes.
evidence_signals: Documentation of proxy removal or adjustment
controls.remediation_template: Systematically identify and test for proxy variables linked to protected attributes. Apply mitigation techniques (e.g., adversarial debiasing, feature removal) and document rationale.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Ensure data integrity, quality, and accuracy are measured
controls.control_text_full: Is there an established mechanism that measures whether the integrity, quality, and accuracy of data collection and its sources have been evaluated and data is up to date?
controls.intent_summary: Continuous monitoring must be in place to verify that data remains accurate, complete, and timely throughout the AI lifecycle.
controls.remediation_template: Implement automated data quality monitoring with thresholds for accuracy, completeness, and timeliness. Trigger alerts and retraining workflows when thresholds are breached.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Review dataset for personal data presence
controls.control_text_full: Has the data been reviewed to check if personal data is evident within the dataset?
controls.intent_summary: All datasets must be screened for the presence of personal or identifiable information to ensure appropriate handling under privacy regulations.
controls.remediation_template: Deploy automated PII detection tools on all datasets and maintain a log of findings. Apply appropriate controls (anonymization, access restrictions) based on classification.
controls.framework_name_ar: مبادئ منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية للذكاء الاصطناعي

related_icaire_terms.term_en: Test Dataset (or Test Data, Test Set)
Validation Dataset (or Validation Data, Validation Set)
